summary: The user sought nutrition advice for post-run recovery after completing a charity 5K in 27 minutes and 12 seconds. They also discussed their training plans for an upcoming half-marathon on December 5th, including incorporating hill repeats and strength training exercises to enhance their running efficiency and endurance.
facts: The user completed a charity 5K run on an unspecified date, finishing with a personal best time of 27 minutes and 12 seconds. The user is planning to participate in a half-marathon on December 5th. The user has been averaging about 15 miles per week in their running routine for the past month. The user has incorporated strength training into their routine, focusing on exercises that target their core and legs. The user is considering adding hill repeats to their speed workouts to improve running efficiency and endurance. The user is looking for specific strength training exercises that can be done at home or at the gym with minimal equipment. The user has been advised to include exercises such as squats, lunges, planks, and glute bridges in their strength training routine. The user has been advised to perform hill repeats on a hill with a grade of 4-6% for optimal training benefits. The user has been advised to warm up with 1-2 miles of easy running before starting hill repeats and to cool down with 1-2 miles of easy running afterward.
